news.headline: RBB Bancorp announces Johnny Lee as new President, effective Jan 1, 2025, and as CEO from May 22, 2025. Leadership succession plan outlined.
news.summary: LOS ANGELES, December 19, 2024 - RBB Bancorp (NASDAQ: RBB) has announced that as of January 1, 2025, Mr. Johnny Lee, the current President and Chief Banking Officer of Royal Business Bank, will assume the role of President of the Company, President and CEO of the Bank, and will also join as a director of both the Company and the Bank. Additionally, starting May 22, 2025, Mr. Lee will take on the position of CEO of the Company. With experience garnered from various financial institutions, Mr. Lee is poised to bring valuable expertise to lead the Company towards further growth and success.

news.headline: AMCON Distributing Company declares $0.28 special dividend to shareholders. Payable on January 31, 2025, to those on record by December 30, 2024.

AMCON, along with its subsidiaries Team Sledd, LLC and Henry’s Foods, Inc., operates as a prominent Convenience and Foodservice Distributor. The announcement came from AMCON's Board of Directors.

news.headline: Expedia Group appoints Scott Schenkel as CFO, effective after filing Form 10-K by Feb 7, 2025. Julie Whalen to remain as CFO until then.
news.summary: Expedia Group (NASDAQ: EXPE) has announced the appointment of Scott Schenkel as Chief Financial Officer, starting the day after the filing of the Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024, expected around February 7, 2025. Julie Whalen will continue as CFO until February to facilitate a seamless transition. Mr. Schenkel will take over the CFO position and oversee financial matters at Expedia Group.

news.headline: Cryptocurrency market plunges - Bitcoin: -5.7%, Ethereum: -9.3%, XRP: -6.5%, Dogecoin: -14.1% on post-FOMC turmoil. Bitcoin falls to $97,083.43. Large transaction volume drops by 7.4%. 368,308 traders liquidated at $1.19 billion. Whales buy $1 billion BTC during dip. Exodus shares rise by 37% on Wall Street debut.
news.summary: Cryptocurrency markets saw a decline on Thursday, as Bitcoin dropped below $100,000 after the recent Federal Reserve meeting. The current prices are as follows: Bitcoin at $97,083.43 (-5.7%), Ethereum at $3,377.75 (-9.3%), Solana at $192.61 (-8.3%), XRP at $2.21 (-6.5%), Dogecoin at $0.312 (-14.1%), and Shiba Inu at $0.00002161 (-12%). Important stats reveal a 7.4% decrease in large transaction volume, with transactions over $100,000 down from 14,557 to 13,516 in a day. A total of 368,308 traders were liquidated, amounting to $1.19 billion, the highest since Dec.10. Additionally, data shows a 4.09% decrease in exchanges netflows. Analyst Ali Martinez highlighted that whales purchased $1 billion worth of BTC during the market dip. Noteworthy news includes crypto wallet provider Exodus making a successful Wall Street debut with shares rising by 37%, and Germany's largest bank reportedly ... (full story available on Benzinga.com).

news.headline: JBM lists 504-unit multifamily portfolio Oasis Naples and Coral Palms in Naples, FL for TE Miller.
news.summary: In Naples, Florida on December 19, 2024, JBM® Institutional Multifamily Advisors announced the exclusive listing of Oasis Naples and Coral Palms for TE Miller. This portfolio includes a total of 504 units and offers significant potential for value enhancement and positive lease tradeouts. Prospective buyers will be provided with the opportunity to explore the investment potential of these properties, which present a promising opportunity for growth.

news.headline: Missouri American Water gives $20K to Urban League of St. Louis for workforce development programs to bridge equality gap.
news.summary: The American Water Charitable Foundation, a non-profit organization founded by American Water, the leading regulated water and wastewater utility company in the U.S., alongside Missouri American Water, disclosed a $20,000 pledge for the Urban League of Metropolitan St. Louis. This grant is a component of a broader Foundation Corporate Strategic funding, aimed at bolstering workforce development programs to address disparities for African Americans and other marginalized communities in the region.

news.headline: Bowman wins contracts for geospatial data & asset management in Texas, worth $900,000 for exploration company.
news.summary: Bowman Consulting Group Ltd., a national engineering services firm listed on NASDAQ as BWMN, based in Reston, Virginia, has recently disclosed securing two notable contracts. These contracts, valued at nearly $900,000 when combined, involve providing asset management and data collection services. The services will be delivered to a prominent exploration and production company operating in the Permian Basin region. Bowman specializes in delivering infrastructure solutions and program management services to clients involved in the ownership, development, and maintenance of the built environment.

news.headline: Republic appoints James Newman as General Partner & VP of Operations, bringing 20 years of experience in finance and tech.
news.summary: Republic, a prominent global investment platform merging finance and technology, has appointed James Newman as General Partner – Fund Manager & Vice President of Operations. With over twenty years of experience in traditional finance, venture development, investing, and expertise in Web3 and digital assets, Newman's leadership is seen as crucial to Republic's operations.

news.headline: Molecular Templates (Nasdaq: MTEM) faces delisting due to being a "public shell," not filing required reports, and failing to maintain bid price. Trading to be suspended on Dec 26, 2024.
news.summary: Molecular Templates, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company on Nasdaq as MTEM, was notified by Nasdaq that it is considered a "public shell" and faced delisting due to non-compliance with filing rules and bid price requirements. Failure to file the September 30, 2024 Form 10-Q and maintain a $1.00 bid price led to the decision. Trading of the company's stock will be suspended on December 26, 2024, unless they appeal and rectify the issues. The company does not plan to appeal and anticipates the suspension to take effect on the specified date.

news.headline: Pyxis Oncology shifts focus to PYX-201, its key clinical program, through portfolio realignment.
U.S. natural gas hits $3.59 per million British units, up 6.5% this week. Rising prices could increase utility bills.
news.summary: U.S. natural gas prices surged on Thursday, with the Henry Hub benchmark leaping 6.5% to $3.59 per million British units, marking the highest close since January 2023. Prices have risen by 9.5% since the beginning of the week, indicating the potential for the eighth week of gains out of the last nine. The recent increase is attributed to strong withdrawals from storage, solid domestic and global demand, and ongoing geopolitical uncertainties in Europe. This upward trend in natural gas prices could lead to challenges with inflation and the economy, possibly resulting in higher utility costs for American consumers in the near future. The rise in natural gas prices compounds concerns following a Federal Reserve meeting this week, where policymakers projected higher inflation for next year and suggested a slower rate of rate cuts. Furthermore, the U.S. Energy Information Administration confirmed a third consecutive triple-digit drop in storage with a reported 125 billion.

news.headline: ASA Gold and Precious Metals Fund has adopted a limited-duration shareholder rights plan. The plan expires on December 20, 2024.
news.summary: In Portland, Maine, the Rights Plan Committee of the Board of Directors of ASA Gold and Precious Metals Limited (NYSE: ASA) has approved a limited-duration shareholder rights plan to safeguard the Company and its shareholders. The current plan is set to end on December 20, 2024. The newly implemented Rights Plan mirrors the existing one significantly.

news.headline: FedEx reports Q2 EPS of $3.03 and adjusted EPS of $4.05. Revenue $22.0B, operating income $1.05B.
news.summary: FedEx Corp. announced its second quarter results for the period ending November 30, with revenue totaling $22.0 billion for both fiscal 2025 and fiscal 2024. Operating income was reported at $1.05 billion (GAAP) and $1.38 billion (adjusted, non-GAAP) for fiscal 2025, while for fiscal 2024, it was $1.28 billion (GAAP) and $1.42 billion (adjusted, non-GAAP). The operating margin stood at 4.8% for fiscal 2025 and 6.3% for fiscal 2024.

news.headline: Curbline Properties declares $0.25 per share special cash dividend, payable on January 16, 2025 to stockholders.
news.summary: Curbline Properties Corp. (NYSE: CURB) based in New York, announced a special cash dividend of $0.25 per share today. This real estate company specializes in owning convenience shopping centers located strategically on curbline of busy intersections and major traffic routes in affluent suburban areas. The dividend is set to be paid on January 16, 2025, to shareholders on record as of December 31, 2024. Curbline Properties focuses on managing these properties to serve the local community effectively.

news.headline: BJ’s Wholesale Club opens 40th club in Florida at St. Johns on December 20, 2024, featuring gas station with low prices.
news.summary: BJ’s Wholesale Club (NYSE: BJ) has revealed plans for the opening of its newest location in St. Johns, Florida on December 20, 2024. Situated at 115 Beachwalk Blvd. in the Beachwalk East Shopping Center, this will mark the 40th club for the company in Florida. The new establishment will include an on-site BJ’s Gas station offering competitive fuel prices and possible additional discounts through the BJ’s Fuel Saver Program. BJ’s is known for providing exceptional value on a wide range of daily necessities, all available in a convenient one-stop shopping experience.

news.headline: FCPT buys P.F. Chang's property for $4.8 million, expanding its net-leased restaurant portfolio.
news.summary: In Mill Valley, California, a real estate investment trust known as Four Corners Property Trust (NYSE:FCPT) has recently acquired a P.F. Chang's property for $4.8 million. The property is a part of the company's portfolio which primarily consists of high-quality, net-leased restaurant and retail properties. P.F. Chang's China Bistro, a popular American casual dining restaurant chain, operates over 200 locations in the United States and offers Asian fusion cuisine. This acquisition highlights FCPT's strategy of investing in well-established properties within the restaurant sector.

news.headline: Mister Car Wash transfers stock listing from NYSE to Nasdaq, effective Dec 31, 2024. Ticker symbol remains "MCW".
news.summary: In Tucson, Arizona, Mister Car Wash, Inc. (NYSE: MCW), the largest car wash brand in the U.S., revealed its decision to shift the listing of its common stock from NYSE to Nasdaq Global Select Market. The transition is set to occur on December 31, 2024, post market closure. Trading under the ticker symbol "MCW", the company anticipates its common stock to commence trading on Nasdaq starting January 2, 2025. This move signifies a significant strategy change for the renowned car wash brand.

The exciting announcement was made on December 19, 2024, in San Diego. This move signifies a shift towards a new stage in advocating and enacting renewable energy practices.

news.headline: Itron and Xcel Energy work together to manage rising DERs in Colorado, incl. residential battery storage, enhancing grid flexibility.
news.summary: Itron and Xcel Energy have joined forces to handle the increasing amount of Distributed Energy Resources (DERs), such as residential battery energy storage systems, in order to bolster grid flexibility.

news.headline: Sight Sciences' TearCare® System for MGD offers cost savings over existing treatments, as per Expert Review of Ophthalmology.
news.summary: An analysis on budget impact was recently released in the Expert Review of Ophthalmology journal. The study delved into the financial implications related to ophthalmology services and treatments. The analysis revealed significant figures regarding the budget impact of such services, shedding light on the costs involved. This publication is expected to provide valuable insights for both healthcare professionals and policymakers in managing finances related to ophthalmic care.

news.headline: 89bio granted 48,000 stock options to new employee under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4) on Dec 13, 2024.
news.summary: On December 13, 2024, 89bio, Inc. (Nasdaq: ETNB), a biopharmaceutical company in San Francisco, approved the granting of 48,000 non-qualified stock options to a new employee as part of an inducement plan. These options allow the purchase of the company's common stock and were issued under the 2023 Inducement Plan by the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors. The grant was made in accordance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4) to incentivize the individual's employment with the company.

news.headline: Ultragenyx doses first patient in Phase 3 study on GTX-102 for Angelman Syndrome, plans to start Aurora study in 2025.
news.summary: The company is progressing towards commencing the Aurora study in 2025 to assess the effectiveness of GTX-102 in various Angelman syndrome genotypes and age groups. This initiative aims to expand the understanding of GTX-102's potential benefits across different segments of individuals affected by the syndrome. The study is anticipated to provide valuable insights into the broader applicability and impact of GTX-102 within the Angelman syndrome community.

news.headline: Mission Produce Inc. reported strong Q4 results with total revenue up 37% to $354.4M, net income of $17.3M, adjusted EBITDA up 113% to $36.9M. For FY 2024, total revenue increased 29% to $1.23B, net income of $36.7M, adjusted EBITDA up 123% to $107.8M. Owned avocado production volume decreased by 60% to 43M pounds. Cash flow from operations was $93.4M compared to $29.2M last year. CEO highlighted positive results and strategic growth initiatives for fiscal 2025.
news.summary: Mission Produce, Inc. ("Mission") reported strong financial results for fiscal Q4 2024 and full-year 2024. In Q4, total revenue rose by 37% to $354.4 million, with net income at $17.3 million compared to $4.0 million in the same period last year. Adjusted net income and EBITDA also saw significant increases. Full-year 2024 revenue increased by 29% to $1.23 billion, with net income at $36.7 million compared to a loss in the prior year. Adjusted net income was $52.8 million, and adjusted EBITDA surged by 123% to $107.8 million. Cash flow from operations for the year was $93.4 million, showing a substantial increase from the prior year. The strong performance was driven by the Marketing & Distribution and Blueberries segments. The CEO attributed the success to their business model and industry-leading position, with a focus on operational excellence and strategic growth initiatives for fiscal 2025. Despite some pricing moderation expected in the avocado market, Mission remains well-positioned for growth. The outlook for fiscal Q1 2025 is positive, with expected price increases and volume growth in certain segments. The company's financial statements revealed a growth in assets, liabilities, and equity, demonstrating a healthy financial position. Non-GAAP measures, such as adjusted net income and adjusted EBITDA, were used to analyze business results. Mission Produce aims to continue its global leadership in the avocado market while expanding into other products like mangos and blueberries.

news.headline: Energy Transfer to supply Chevron 2M tonnes of LNG annually from Lake Charles facility under 20-year agreement.
news.summary: In Dallas, Energy Transfer LP (NYSE: ET) revealed that its subsidiary, Energy Transfer LNG Export, LLC, has signed a 20-year LNG Sale and Purchase Agreement (SPA) with Chevron U.S.A. Inc. pertaining to the Lake Charles LNG project. As per the deal, Energy Transfer LNG will provide Chevron with 2.0 million tonnes of LNG annually on a free-on-board (FOB) basis. The purchase price will include a fixed liquefaction component.

news.headline: CenterPoint Energy offers solution to ERCOT shortfall: 15 large units to San Antonio for 2 yrs in 2025, no profit, lower bills by Spring 2025.
news.summary: A proposition has been made to allocate all 15 major emergency generation units to the San Antonio area for a duration of two years commencing in Spring 2025. The company behind this proposal has clarified that they will not derive any revenue or profit from this initiative, aiming to assist ERCOT and the state in managing power supply challenges. As a result, customers in the Greater Houston Area can expect reductions in their bills starting from Spring 2025.
